summary.headline: ADM reports fourth quarter 2022 earnings per share of $1.84, adjusted EPS of $1.93, and increases quarterly dividend by 12.5%.
summary.narrative_summary: ADM announced its fourth quarter and annual 2022 financial results, reporting earnings per share of $1.84 and adjusted EPS of $1.93. The company delivered strong performance across segments, with notable growth in Ag Services and Oilseeds and Carbohydrate Solutions. The company also announced a 12.5% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.45 per share, marking its 50th consecutive year of dividend increases. Financial highlights include adjusted segment operating profit of $1.665 billion and trailing four-quarter average adjusted ROIC of 13.6%.

drivers: strong export volumes for soybean meal
growing domestic demand for renewable diesel
low water conditions reduced North American export volumes
South American team executed well to deliver higher margins and volumes
concerns: higher energy costs in EMEA compared to prior year
expanding margins drove negative timing impacts of approximately $40 million

drivers: Starches and Sweeteners subsegment delivered much higher year-over-year results
North America business delivered solid volumes and strong margins in both starches and sweeteners
EMEA team effectively managed risk and delivered improved results on better margins
concerns: Vantage Corn Processors results were substantially lower due to higher ethanol inventory levels pressuring margins
global wheat milling business delivered higher margins driven by solid customer demand

drivers: Human Nutrition results were lower than those of the fourth quarter of 2021
Flavors results were similar to the prior year, as strong revenue growth helped offset demand fulfillment challenges
Specialty Ingredients continued to see strong demand for its product portfolio, including plant-based proteins
Health Wellness was higher year over year, driven primarily by the bioactives portfolio, including the results from the Deerland acquisition
concerns: Animal Nutrition results were substantially lower than the prior-year quarter, primarily due to lower margins in amino acids driven by recovery in the global supply of lysine
pet nutrition volumes were lower in Latin America, partially driven by demand fulfillment challenges
Feed results were stronger, driven by APAC and Latin America, partially offset by the impact of softer demand in EMEA

drivers: Higher short-term interest rates drove improved earnings in ADM Investor Services
Captive insurance experienced favorable underwriting results and lower claim settlements versus the prior year

ma_or_capital_allocation.description: ADM increased its quarterly dividend by 12.5% to $0.45 per share.

extraction_notes: Numbers extracted from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references. Some segment commentary was inferred from narrative descriptions.
